name: Patient G
presenting_complaint: Intermittent left-sided chest discomfort over the last 12 hours, worse on exertion, eased with rest; came in only because his wife insisted.
history: Type 2 diabetes on insulin, hyperlipidaemia on atorvastatin, prior PCI 4 years ago to LAD. Reports 'just a 2/10 pressure, probably indigestion.' AVPU=A. Walking comfortably at present.

trajectory.state: Comfortable at rest, NRS 2/10. ECG sinus rhythm, no acute changes. Initial troponin pending.
Walks to the toilet and re-presents with 5/10 chest pressure. ECG now shows new T-wave inversion in V4-V6, troponin still negative.
If managed with antiplatelet + anticoagulant + cardiology referral: pain settles, admitted for inpatient angiography.
If under-triaged and discharged: high risk of progressing to NSTEMI or out-of-hospital arrest within 24-48h.

red_herrings: Patient repeatedly says 'I think it's just heartburn'
Initial ECG and first troponin are normal — easy to discharge
